framework.name: Credentials Provider as Liability Firewall
framework.description: The CP role separates the agent that shops from the entity that pays. The Shopping Agent never touches PCI data or PII. This architectural decision implicitly answers the delegation-depth liability question — the CP is the choke point. If Agent C overspends, the CP should have blocked it because it controls credential release, not the agents in the chain.
framework.raw_quote: agents involved in the shopping process are prevented from accessing sensitive payment card industry (PCI) data or other personally identifiable information (PII), which is handled exclusively by the specialized entities

source.title: AP2 specification - AP2 - Agent Payments Protocol Documentation
